justification: All pass criteria met: stripe wildcard identified as Critical; jsonwebtoken CVE flagged as Critical with specific CVE number; all 5 of 5 risks identified (stripe, jsonwebtoken, crypto-random-string, left-pad, moment); safe_to_deploy is false; severities are differentiated (Critical for stripe/jwt/esm, Important for left-pad/moment/lockfile, Suggestions separate).

observations.text: Stripe wildcard correctly identified as Critical with detailed explanation of payment risk
jsonwebtoken CVE-2022-23529 cited by number with accurate description of the auth-bypass vulnerability
crypto-random-string ESM incompatibility identified with correct technical detail (ERR_REQUIRE_ESM)
left-pad supply chain risk noted with historical context
moment.js deprecation flagged with migration alternatives
Bonus: identified missing package-lock.json as an additional risk
Reproduction steps and fix suggestions are concrete and actionable

crypto-random-string rated Critical rather than Important — slightly over-classified given it would fail at startup (visible) rather than silently, though the reasoning about payment context is defensible
